title: Instrument d'acceptation de l'Australie
text_preview: WHEREAS the Convention Relating to International Exhibitions was signed at Paris on the twenty-second day of November, One thousand nine hundred and twenty-eight, and supplemented by the protocols of 10 May 1948, 16 November 1966 and 30 November 1972; and WHEREAS the General Assembly of the International Exhibitions Bureau adopted on the twenty-fourth of June, One thousand nine hundred and eighty-two, in accordance…

pages.text:
WHEREAS the Convention Relating to International

Exhibitions was signed at Paris on the twenty-second day of

November, One thousand nine hundred and twenty-eight, and supplemented by the protocols of 10 May 1948, 16 November 1966 and 30 November 1972; and

WHEREAS the General Assembly of the International

Exhibitions Bureau adopted on the twenty-fourth of June, One thousand nine hundred and eighty-two, in accordance with paragraph 3 of Article 28, a proposal by the Government of the

French Republic to amend paragraph 2 of Article 5; and

WHEREAS the Government of Australia is a Contracting

Party to the Convention; and

WHEREAS Governments Parties to the Convention may, in accordance with paragraph 3 of Article 33, accept the amendment:

THE GOVERNMENT OF AUSTRALIA having considered the amendment hereby ACCEPTS the same.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I, WILLIAM GEORGE HAYDEN, Minister of State for Foreign Affairs have hereunto set my hand and affixed my seal.

DONE at

R12:0370901

• One thousand nine hundred and eighty-three.

Minister of state for foreign Affairs of Australia
